calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-14-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-14-2018 Continued To May-30-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Jaret, Esq. 415-455-1010 of Jaret & Jaret for Plaintiff Juan Jose Artega through Court Call.
Curtis Herron, Esq. 714-794-8001 for Defendant CHANG LIU, XINFENG HU through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues fourth cause of action for Fraud and punitive damages. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHANG LIU, XINFENG HU'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Chang Liu and Xinfeng Hu's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations and prayers in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Juan Arteaga is Granted with 20 days leave to amend for Mr. Artega to adequately allege his fourth cause of action for fraud claims.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Jaret, Esq. 415-455-1010 of Jaret & Jaret for Plaintiff Juan Jose Artega through Court Call.
Curtis Herron, Esq. 714-794-8001 for Defendant CHANG LIU, XINFENG HU through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues fourth cause of action for Fraud and punitive damages. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHANG LIU, XINFENG HU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Chang Liu and Xinfeng Hu's demurrer to the first, fourth and fifth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Juan Arteaga is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to first and fifth causes of action for breach of contract and fraud by false promise and sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for fraud by intentional misrepresentation.

Mr. Artega has leave to amend the breach of contract claim to clarify the precise contract price and what he is alleged owed. Mr. Artega has leave to amend the false promise claim to allege with specificity when the promises alleged in paragraphs 33-34 were made and whether they were verbal or written. The economic loss rule does not bar Mr. Artega's fraud claims. (Robinson Helicopter Co. v. Dana Corp. (2004) 34 Cal.4th 979, 991.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
